title: Geometrization of the local Langlands correspondence, motivically

thm:1.1.summary: The Fargues–Scholze parameter $\varphi_{\pi,\iota}$ attached to an irreducible representation $\pi$ is independent of the choice of $\ell$ and isomorphism $\iota$ — resolving the $\ell$-independence of the correspondence via a motivic refinement.

thm:1.2.summary: Fargues–Scholze Conjecture I.9.5 holds for every reductive $G$: the map from the excursion (spectral Bernstein-center) algebra to $G(\mathbb{Q}_p)$-representations is independent of $\ell$ and $\iota$ as a refinement of the geometric Langlands correspondence.

thm:3.1.summary: The motivic category $\mathcal{D}_{\mathrm{mot}}(\mathrm{Bun}_G)$: the Harder–Narasimhan charts $\pi_b: \mathcal{M}_b \to \mathrm{Bun}_G$ are cohomologically smooth, giving an excision/semiorthogonal decomposition along $B(G)$ — the motivic lift of the Fargues–Scholze sheaf theory on $\mathrm{Bun}_G$.

def:4.1.summary: The motivic coefficient system $\mathrm{MG}$ over $\mathrm{Div}^1$: a motivic refinement of the unit coefficients, the device that makes the construction $\ell$-independent by working with motivic rather than $\ell$-adic sheaves.

thm:5.6.summary: The motivic Hecke (universally locally acyclic) category is stable under constant-term functors — the geometric input that lets Satake descend to motivic coefficients.

thm:5.7.summary: Motivic geometric Satake: for each finite set $I$, the Satake category $\mathrm{Sat}_G^I$ is equivalent to representations of $\widehat{G}^I$ on vector bundles over $\mathrm{MG}_{\mathrm{Div}^I}$ — the motivic construction of the Langlands dual group and the spectral action.

cor:6.3.summary: The independence-of-$\ell$ conjecture (Fargues–Scholze Conjecture I.9.5) holds — the synthesis of the motivic geometric Satake and spectral action, specialized back to $\ell$-adic coefficients.

edges.note: The parameter's $\ell$-independence comes from constructing it through the motivic geometric Satake / dual-group action, which is manifestly $\ell$-independent.
The excursion / spectral-action statement is read off the motivic geometric Satake equivalence.
The argument runs on the motivic category $\mathcal{D}_{\mathrm{mot}}(\mathrm{Bun}_G)$ and its strata decomposition.
The formal independence-of-$\ell$ corollary is the specialization of Theorem 1.2.
Motivic Satake is built from the constant-term-stable motivic Hecke category.
The Satake equivalence is stated over the motivic coefficient system $\mathrm{MG}_{\mathrm{Div}^I}$.
The motivic $\mathrm{Bun}_G$ category is defined with motivic coefficients.

motivic_infrastructure.description: The motivic coefficient system and the motivic category on $\mathrm{Bun}_G$.

motivic_geometric_satake.description: Motivic Hecke category and the motivic geometric Satake equivalence constructing the dual group / spectral action over motivic coefficients.

main_results.description: $\ell$-independence of the Fargues–Scholze parameter and the resolution of Conjecture I.9.5.

FarguesScholze.title: Geometrization of the local Langlands correspondence
FarguesScholze.role: the construction this paper refines to motivic coefficients; Conjecture I.9.5 (independence of $\ell$) is the target, proved by the motivic geometric Satake / spectral action
